contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT] - Type: Time (Minutes) - Amount: 30 - Description: Two Bahia bars newsletter — pipeline upgrades + live send to 38 subscribers Pull requests (GitHub evidence): - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/79 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/81 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/84 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/85 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/53 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/63 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/66 Details: ## What changed Shipped the first end-to-end Agroverse newsletter campaign — `two_bahia_bars` — covering Kirsten's pair of 81% single-estate dark chocolate bars (Oscar's Farm 2024 + Fazenda Santa Ana 2023). Five review iterations with feedback applied at each step, then a live send to 38 confirmed subscribers. Pipeline gained four reusable upgrades along the way that future newsletters inherit. ### Pipeline upgrades shipped - **`send_newsletter.py` default-on tracking + buyer exclusion + image markdown** ([go_to_market #79](https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/79)). `--track-opens` / `--track-clicks` flipped to `BooleanOptionalAction(default=True)` so a forgotten flag can no longer silently produce an untracked send. New `--exclude-buyers-of-substring TEXT` (repeatable) reads the canonical `Agroverse QR codes` tab and drops any recipient whose email holds the campaign's SKU. Markdown converter extended to recognize standard `![alt](src)` image syntax (HTML emits responsive ``; plain text emits `[image: alt]`). - **Compact images + farm URL swap** ([go_to_market #81](https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/81)). Default img width 480 → 320 after operator review. - **JPG fallback for AGL14 + tighter margin** ([go_to_market #84](https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/84)). AVIF doesn't decode in Outlook desktop and several mobile clients; converted to JPG and committed alongside the source AVIF in [truesight_me_beta #53](https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/53). Width 320 → 280, margin 18 → 4 since the surrounding `

` block already provides the breathing room. - **Side-by-side comparison row + HTML-aware plain-text** ([go_to_market #85](https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/85)). Compact two-card row at the top of the email with farm name, vintage chip, three-word taste essence, and CTA. Side-by-side on desktop, stacks on mobile via `display:inline-block + max-width:100%`. `markdown_to_plain` hardened to handle `` and strip remaining HTML so the comparison row degrades cleanly for non-HTML clients. ### Documentation shipped - **§4.3a "Buyer exclusion — don't pitch a SKU to someone who already owns it"** added to `AGROVERSE_NEWSLETTER_WORKFLOW.md` ([agentic_ai_context #63](https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/63)). Codifies the rule, the source-of-truth schema (`Agroverse QR codes` cols D/I/L), how to pick a stable substring, the worked example with exact flag values, and when to broaden the status filter. - **`OPEN_FOLLOWUPS.md` entry** for the post-send read-out ([agentic_ai_context #66](https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/66)). Tracks the open / click / reply analysis the campaign needs after a 10-day soak window (earliest 2026-05-07), including the specific design question to answer: did the comparison-row CTA get clicked at a different rate than the in-section CTAs? ### Operational work - Subscriber-sheet hygiene: 17 obvious test addresses (Wix integration tests, `@garyteh.com` / `@org.com` / `@cors.org` patterns) flipped from `CONFIRMED` to `TEST` to drop them from the live send. Verified zero QR-code emails were missing from the subscriber list (existing import pipeline keeps pace). - Four review-round drafts sent to `garyjob@gmail.com` (v2, v3, v4, v5) before the live send to gather operator feedback at each design iteration. - Live send: 38 recipients drafted and delivered, 2 buyers correctly excluded via the new mechanism (`pamelacotton7@msn.com`, `toffees_fibrils.0l@icloud.com` — both already hold one of the bars per `Agroverse QR codes` SOLD rows). Tracking on, label `Newsletter/2 Chocolate Bars`, all 38 logged to `Agroverse News Letter Emails` for open/click follow-up. ## Why Every upgrade in this batch was driven by something that surfaced during review: - Tracking-on default was a direct response to "the Agroverse logo (used for tracking) needs to be included" — same shape as the warm-up pipeline fix in PR #75. - Buyer exclusion came from "we should not generate the draft for folks who have already bought the bars before." - Image-rendering work (AVIF→JPG, then size/margin tightening) responded to "image not displaying" + "feels cluttered" iterating until the layout breathed. - The side-by-side comparison row was the operator's idea and reinforces the "very different chocolate" thesis at a glance. Net: pipeline is meaningfully more robust (tracking can't be forgotten, buyers can't be re-pitched), documentation captures the rule for future agents, and the campaign itself reached the right 38 people without spamming the 2 who already bought. ## Pull requests - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/79 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/81 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/84 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/85 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/53 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/63 - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/66 - Contributor(s): Garyjob - TDG Issued: 50.00 - Attached Filename: N/A - Destination Contribution File Location: N/A -------- My Digital Signature:  Request Transaction ID:  This submission was generated using https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/blob/main/DAO_CLIENT_AI_AGENT_CONTRIBUTIONS.md Verify submission here: https://dapp.truesight.me/verify_request.html
rubric: 100TDG For every 1 hour of human effort
